hypothesis: Baseline run without changes (+ GQA fix for PyTorch 2.4 compat)
reason: Baseline on A40. 73 steps in 2 min (~1656ms/step). 5.5MB compressed.

hypothesis: SwiGLU MLP instead of relu^2 (gate+up+down at hidden=2*dim)
reason: Worse: +0.067 bpb. More params = slower steps = fewer iterations.

hypothesis: Reduce layers from 9 to 6 for faster steps and more iterations
reason: Big improvement: -0.436 bpb! 50% more steps (109 vs 73), 33% faster (1103ms/step). Fewer params but much more training = better. 4.2MB compressed.

hypothesis: H100 baseline with 6 layers (same config as exp_003)
reason: H100 baseline. 296 steps in 2 min (~406ms/step). 4x more steps than A40. 5.8MB compressed.

hypothesis: 4 layers on H100 (fewer layers = more steps)
reason: -0.118 bpb vs 6 layers. 395 steps at 305ms/step. Trend continues on H100.

hypothesis: 3 layers on H100
reason: Marginal -0.006 bpb vs 4 layers. Diminishing returns. 444 steps at 271ms/step. 3.5MB.

hypothesis: 2 layers on H100 — find inflection point
reason: Worse: +0.035 bpb vs 3 layers. Too few layers = insufficient capacity. Optimal is 3-4 layers for 2-min H100 runs.

hypothesis: 4 layers, dim=768, 12 heads — wider model
reason: Much worse. Too wide = too slow (423ms/step, 284 steps). 16.5M params. Steps lost outweigh capacity gained.

hypothesis: 3 layers, dim=640 — slightly wider
reason: Worse than 3L/512d (1.58). Extra width slows steps (398 vs 444). dim=512 is the sweet spot for 2-min runs.

hypothesis: 3 layers × 2 loops = 6 effective depth (looped layers)
reason: Worse. Looping doubles compute/step (385ms vs 271ms), losing 132 steps. Shared weights don't help at short training.

hypothesis: 3 layers, matrix_lr=0.08 (2x default)
reason: -0.029 bpb vs default LR. Higher LR helps convergence in short training. Same speed.

hypothesis: 3 layers, matrix_lr=0.12 (3x default)
reason: Marginal -0.001 bpb vs lr=0.08. Near LR optimum around 0.08-0.12.

hypothesis: 3 layers, lr=0.10, batch=262144 (smaller batch for more steps)
reason: Worse than default batch. More steps (467) but less informative per step.

hypothesis: 9 layers, original LR=0.04, 10 min on H100 — test capacity with enough training time
reason: Big win: 1.36 bpb. 9 layers with 10 min much better than 3 layers with 2 min. 12.4MB compressed (under 16MB). Still 0.14 above 1.22 target.

hypothesis: 9 layers, lr=0.10, 10 min — higher LR with long training
reason: Same bpb as lr=0.04 (1.358 vs 1.360). Higher LR doesn't help with 10min training. Compressed 14.6MB — near limit.

hypothesis: 12 layers, lr=0.04, 10 min — more capacity, check if fits 16MB
reason: Worse than 9L (1.373 vs 1.358). Slower steps (807ms) → fewer steps (744 vs 990). 15.1MB near limit. 9 layers optimal for 1xH100/10min.

hypothesis: 6 layers, lr=0.04, 10 min — complete 10-min layer sweep
reason: Same bpb as 9L (1.357 vs 1.358) but only 9.5MB vs 12.4MB. 6.5MB headroom! 1492 steps at 402ms/step. 6L is better: same quality, much smaller.

hypothesis: 9L seq_len=4096 batch=393216 10min — competition says biggest improvement
reason: Marginal -0.006 bpb vs seq1024. On 1xH100 fewer total tokens (348M vs 519M) offset context benefit. Should help more on 8xH100. 11.9MB compressed.

hypothesis: 9L seq4096 + tuned Muon (lr=0.02, mom=0.99, warmdown=3000) — competition params
reason: WORSE. Competition Muon tuning (lower LR, higher momentum, longer warmdown) needs 9500 steps. With only 890 steps on 1xH100, underfits. Stick with default Muon for 1xH100.

hypothesis: 9L seq4096 10min + sliding window eval stride=512 (testing slide eval)
reason: Same training as exp_018 (1.3525). Sliding window eval too slow on 1xH100 (killed after >10min). Code ready for 8xH100 final. Standard eval: 1.3546 roundtrip.
